recalls.Description: Sanven Technology has announced a recall for Vevor Baby Swings due to a serious suffocation hazard. These swings, available in white/gray and black/gray, feature the "VEVOR" trademark and are marked with the model number on the bottom or back of the base. The specific models involved are BB501K (white/gray with a white base), BB702A (white/gray with a gray base), and BB005 (black/gray with a black base).
recalls.Title: Sanven Technology Issues Recall for Vevor Baby Swings Due to Suffocation Risk; Noncompliance with Infant Sleep Product Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the swings immediately and reach out to Sanven Technology for a full refund. Contact them toll-free at 855-599-6320 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recalling@vevor.com, or visit https://www.vevor.com/pages/vevor-baby-swing-recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Vevor Baby Swing in White/Gray with White Base and Mosquito Net, Model Number BB501
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing Label on White Base, Model Number BB501K
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing in White/Gray with Gray Base, Model Number BB702A
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing Label on Gray Base, Model Number BB702A
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing in Black/Gray with Black Base, Sun Visor, and Mosquito Net, Model Number BB005K
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing in Black/Gray with Black Base, Model Number BB005K
Recalled Vevor Baby Swing Label on Black Base, Model Number BB005K

Retailers.Name: Available at Vevor.com and Amazon.com from January 2025 to August 2025, priced between $65 and $80.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, violating safety standards for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings, contact Sanven Technology for a refund, destroy the swing cover, and dispose of the swing per local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Sanven Technology recalls Vevor Baby Swings due to suffocation risks. Immediate action required for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Peg Perego is recalling its Tatamia 3-in-1 recliners, swings, and high chairs due to a potential suffocation risk. These versatile products can be used as a recliner, high chair, or unpowered infant swing and were sold in a gray color with the model number IMTATANA01BL73. The brand name 'Peg Perego' is displayed on the padded leather seat and the front of the tray in high chair mode. Only items manufactured on or after June 23, 2022, and purchased on or after November 12, 2022, are affected by this recall. The manufacture date and model number can be located on the base of the product.
recalls.Title: Peg Perego Recalls Tatamia 3-in-1 Recliners, Swings, and High Chairs Due to Suffocation Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Peg Perego at 877-737-3464 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email service@pegperego.com, or visit https://www.pegperego.com/en_us/baby/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Peg Perego Tatamia 3-in-1 Recliner, Swing, and High Chair

Images.Caption: Recalled Peg Perego Tatamia 3-in-1 in High Chair Mode
Recalled Peg Perego Tatamia 3-in-1 in Swing Mode
Recalled Peg Perego Tatamia 3-in-1 in Recliner Mode
Label with Date of Manufacture and Model Number Found on Base of Product

Retailers.Name: Available online at PegPerego.com, Amazon.com, Target.com, and other retailers from November 2022 to January 2025, priced around $450.

Importers.Name: Peg Perego USA Inc., of Fort Wayne, Indiana

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper incline angle and failure to meet safety standards.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product immediately and contact Peg Perego for a full refund and return shipping.

recalls.metaDescription: Peg Perego recalls Tatamia 3-in-1 recliners, swings, and high chairs due to suffocation risks. Contact for refunds and more information.

recalls.Description: ZRWD is recalling its infant swings, model number S0008A, due to serious safety concerns. These swings, available in gray and white fabric with a black base, and blue and white fabric with a white base, have an incline greater than 10 degrees, which poses a suffocation risk. They also lack necessary warnings regarding the included lithium CR 2025 3v button cell battery, violating safety standards.
recalls.Title: ZRWD Infant Swings Recalled Due to Suffocation Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to ZRWD via email at zrwdrecall@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Gray and White Fabric and Black Base
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Gray and White Fabric, Black Base and Plush Toys
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Blue and White Fabric and White Base
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing with Blue and White Fabric, White Base and Plush Toys
Recalled ZRWD Infant Swing Remote Control

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from December 2023 to December 2024 for approximately $110.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper incline and missing safety warnings for button cell batteries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings immediately and contact ZRWD for a full refund. Consumers must send a photo of the cut swing cover and the swing marked 'Recalled' to zrwdrecall@163.com before disposing of the product.

recalls.metaDescription: ZRWD recalls infant swings due to suffocation hazards and safety violations. Immediate action required for a full refund.

recalls.Description: Nezmdi is recalling its electric infant swings due to a significant risk of suffocation and serious injury. These swings, designed for infant sleep, have an incline exceeding the Safe Sleep for Babies Act limit of 10 degrees, and they do not meet essential warning requirements. Additionally, the included remote control has a lithium coin battery but lacks necessary safety warnings as mandated by Reese's Law. The swings are available in pink with a white base and feature plush toys, a pillow, and a mosquito net with a canopy.
recalls.Title: Nezmdi Electric Infant Swings Recalled Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Nezmdi via email at yingpengds@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Nezmdi infant swing
A leather patch with the brand Nezmdi located on restraints
"Model Number: S0008" and "Baby Swing Chair" are printed on a label located under the seat
Recalled Nezmdi infant swing remote control

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from September 2024 through February 2025 for approximately $70.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard and risk of serious injury or death due to non-compliance with safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings immediately and contact Nezmdi for a full refund. Send a photo of the swing cover cut in half with 'Recalled' marked on its base to yingpengds@163.com and dispose of the swing according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Nezmdi recalls electric infant swings due to suffocation risks and safety violations. Contact for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description:
The recall affects electric infant swings branded as COMEONROA, which feature a blue space-themed design and a black base. These swings include a touch-screen panel, plush toys shaped like a star and a moon, a pillow, seat cover, and a mosquito net with a canopy. Measuring approximately 25.5 inches in length, 25.5 inches in width, and 20.8 inches in height, they come with a remote control and USB cable. The label under the seat indicates 'Model No.: S-Y608' and 'Baby cradle swing.'

These swings pose a significant suffocation hazard due to their incline exceeding 10 degrees and their marketing for infant sleep, violating the Safe Sleep for Babies Act. Additionally, they do not meet essential warning requirements under current safety standards, lacking necessary warnings related to button cell and coin batteries as mandated by Reese's Law.
recalls.Title: COMEONROA Electric Infant Swings Recalled Due to Suffocation Risk and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled swings and reach out to Eonroacoo at eonroacoorecall@126.com for a full refund. They are required to send a photo of the swing cover cut in half and another photo of the swing with 'Recalled' marked on its base. The swings must be disposed of according to local regulations. Eonroacoo and Walmart will directly contact all known purchasers.

Images.Caption: Recalled COMEONROA infant swing
Recalled COMEONROA infant swing
"Model No.: S-Y608" and "Baby cradle swing" are printed on a label located under the seat

Retailers.Name: Available on Walmart.com from January 2024 to February 2025 for approximately $80.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees and marketing for infant sleep, violating safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the swings, contact Eonroacoo for a refund, and follow disposal instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of COMEONROA electric infant swings due to suffocation risks and safety violations. Contact for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: HONEYJOY is recalling several models of their convertible and foldable high chairs made from metal and plastic, featuring a cloth seat in various colors and prints. These high chairs have been identified as posing a suffocation hazard due to their incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, which violates federal regulations for infant sleep products. The affected models include different versions of the HONEY JOY high chairs, each with specific model numbers found on the sewn-in label on the seat's top cover.
recalls.Title: HONEYJOY Recalls Convertible and Foldable High Chairs Due to Suffocation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using these high chairs immediately and reach out to HONEYJOY at honeyjoyrecall@hotmail.com or visit https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/A401562A-3E1E-43A1-8F23-3AAE28BA14FC?ingress=2&visitId=596d5ffc-9ec0-4624-baae-6aad8d0c70dd&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&ref_=ast_bln for a full refund. Instructions for disposal will be provided by HONEYJOY.

Images.Caption: Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-GR in gray
Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-PI in pink
Recalled HONEYJOY High Chair model AD10025PI in pink

Retailers.Name: Exclusively available online at Amazon.com from April 2023 to August 2024, priced between $79 and $220.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, violating federal regulations for infant sleep products.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the high chairs, contact HONEYJOY for a refund, and deface the product as instructed.

recalls.metaDescription: HONEYJOY recalls high chairs due to suffocation risks. Immediate action required for refunds. Check details here.

recalls.Description: Fisher-Price has issued a recall for its Snuga Infant Swings, which feature both front-to-back and side-to-side motion. These swings rotate 90 degrees, have two adjustable seat back positions, and are designed for easy storage. The recall affects 21 different models that share a similar design, with variations in fabric colors and toy accessories. Model numbers can be found printed on the underside of the swing's motor housing.
recalls.Title: Fisher-Price Recalls Over 2 Million Snuga Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Risk Following 5 Reported Deaths
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Fisher-Price toll-free at 855-853-6224, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.service.mattel.com under "Recall & Safety Alerts" or directly at www.service.mattel.com/us/recall.aspx.

Products.Name: Fisher-Price My Little Snugakitty™ Cradle 'n Swing
Products.NumberOfUnits: Approximately 2.1 million swings were sold in the United States, about 99,000 swings were sold in Canada and about 500 swings were sold in Mexico

Images.Caption: Recalled My Little Snugabunny Cradle 'n Swing (V0099)
Recalled My Little Snugabear Cradle 'n Swing (CHM56)
Recalled Snow Leopard Swing (HBM23)
Recalled My Little SnugaMonkey™ Cradle 'n Swing (X7051)
Recalled My Little Snugapuppy™ Cradle 'n Swing (X7345)
Recalled My Little Snugabear Cradle 'n Swing (X7347)
Representative recalled Snuga Swing headrest
Representative recalled Snuga Swing body support insert

Injuries.Name: Between 2012 and 2022, there have been reports of five deaths involving infants 1 to 3 months of age when the product was used for sleep. In most of those incidents, the infants were unrestrained and bedding materials were added to the product.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon, Toys R Us, Walmart/Sams Club, and Target across the U.S. from October 2010 to January 2024, priced around $160.

Distributors.Name: Fisher-Price of East Aurora, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of suffocation if used for sleep or with bedding materials added.

Remedies.Name: Remove the headrest and body support insert from the seat pad. A $25 refund will be provided for consumers who destroy these items. Instructions are available at www.service.mattel.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Fisher-Price recalls over 2 million Snuga Infant Swings due to suffocation risks. Learn how to get a refund and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: The recall affects Papablic Archie Infant Swings, identified by model number PAPABLIC61A and batch numbers PF2106C01 and PF2301C01. These swings are approximately 25 inches in all dimensions and are gray in color, featuring three plush toys: a star, a moon, and a koala bear. They also include an electronic remote control for operation. The product label on the back confirms its identity as the "Archie Infant Swing."
recalls.Title: Recall of Papablic Archie Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Risk and Federal Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Shenzhen Two Pony at 855-214-2723, available from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email info@papablic.com or visit www.papablic.com/pages/recall-information or click on the "Recall" section on www.papablic.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Papablic Archie Swing Model PAPABLIC61A
Label located on back of swing, showing model and batch information

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and Papablic.com from July 2023 to April 2024, priced around $150.

Importers.Name: Shenzhen Two Pony Tech Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper design for infant sleep, exceeding the safe incline angle, and violations of federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the swings immediately and contact Shenzhen Two Pony for disposal instructions and proof of destruction to receive a full refund. Notifications are being sent to all known purchasers by Shenzhen Two Pony and Amazon.

recalls.metaDescription: Papablic Archie Infant Swings recalled for suffocation hazards and federal regulation violations. Immediate action required for refund.

recalls.Description: Jool Baby is recalling its Nova Baby infant swings, manufactured between June 2022 and September 2023. These swings are gray, measuring approximately 28 inches long, 19 inches wide, and 24 inches high. They feature a round aluminum base with music buttons, a metal seat frame, a cloth seat with restraints and a headrest, and a canopy adorned with hanging toys shaped like a yellow moon, blue cloud, and pink star. The swings can be powered by AAA batteries or an AC adapter and include an electronic remote control. The brand name "Jool Baby" is located on the swing's base, while "NOVA" is found on the seat label. The manufacturing date code is indicated on the sewn-in warning label on the back of the seat.
recalls.Title: Jool Baby Recalls Nova Baby Infant Swings Due to Suffocation Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled swings for sleep immediately and contact Jool Baby at 888-788-5665 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, and from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. ET on Friday. They can also email recall@JoolBaby.com or visit www.JoolBaby.com/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Jool Baby Nova Baby Infant Swing

Images.Caption: Recalled Jool Baby Nova Baby Infant Swing
Model JBP-NOVA-GRY and the manufacture date are printed on the sewn-in warning label on the back of the swing's seat
New seat warning label to be provided as part of the repair kit

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores and online at www.JoolBaby.com, www.amazon.com, www.babylist.com, www.target.com, www.walmart.com, and other retailers from November 2022 to November 2023 for approximately $150.

Importers.Name: Jool Products LLC, dba Jool Baby, of Lakewood, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to improper design for infant sleep and non-compliance with safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including a new seat with updated warnings, plush toys, and a remote control.

recalls.metaDescription: Jool Baby recalls Nova Baby infant swings due to suffocation hazards. Learn how to get a free repair kit and ensure safety.
